extraction.one_line_summary: PETERSON Mads won the sprint from a caught breakaway after CLARK and DE MARCHI waited too long inside final 200m.
tactical_patterns: CLARK/DE MARCHI broke clear on second categorized climb but hesitated fatally at 200m, caught by sprint trains.
INEOS disorganization visible: THOMAS mechanical at 15km cost GC positioning despite team car relay effort.
Break maintained 1+ minute at 15km but unorganized peloton only caught with 200m remaining, not standard catch timing.

rider_signals.CLARK Simon: negative: 36-year-old showed climbing strength to drop three breakaway companions but catastrophic hesitation cost grand tour stage set completion
rider_signals.CAVENDISH Mark: negative: dropped on first categorized climb due to knee injury from previous crash, sprinting form compromised
rider_signals.PETERSON Mads: positive: completed Peter Slam (stages at all three Grand Tours), timing shows tactical maturity against Milan's earlier launches
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: neutral: launched sprint early but held second, retained maglia ciclamino despite missing intermediate sprint points to Groves
